cards.front: What is a tree in data structures?
cards.back: A tree is a non-linear data structure consisting of nodes with a value and zero or more child nodes.
A binary tree is a tree in which each node has at most two children (i.e., left child and right child).

cards.front: What are the three main traversals in a binary tree?
cards.back: The three main traversals in a binary tree are Inorder Traversal, Preorder Traversal, and Postorder Traversal.

cards.front: What is a threaded binary tree?
cards.back: A threaded binary tree is a binary tree where each node has a pointer to the next node in the thread (i.e., left child) or null if there is no next node.

cards.front: What is the main difference between a binary tree and a threaded binary tree?
cards.back: The main difference is that in a threaded binary tree, each node has a pointer to the next node in the thread, whereas in a regular binary tree, each node has child pointers for the left and right subtrees.
